title: Official Name for Third Impact 01
wikitext:
[]: Official Name for Third Impact 01?             Quote Post by Furu » Sun Mar 16, 2008 7:48 am So I've been playing around with my Super Robot Wars games lately (part of the reason I was inspired to google "Eva wiki" and sign up here actually) and after coming to the End of Evangelion bit in Alpha 3, I noticed that the game actually gave a special name to the Third Impact version of EVA-01 with the Spear and the whole cross thing/wings of light. The translation of the name I have is "Yorishiro EVA-01". A quick bit of google research shows that it's a Shinto reference: The place (or object or person) inhabited by a kami's spirit when it descends for a religious ceremony or when possessing a person.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Wed Jun 25, 2008 8:28 am This is worth looking at. What spirit has EVA-01 become a vessel for during 3I?

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Jun 25, 2008 8:58 am heh, in b4 rampant "Yui became Seed of Life" theories

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Wed Jun 25, 2008 10:12 am afaik the entire Seed of Life stuff rests on Reichu's translation of something from a videogame. Who knows what was changed during the translation or whether there is some, intentional or not, bias in leaning towards one interpretation in the translated text.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jun 25, 2008 11:34 am There really aren't many ways one can translate Seimei no Tane （生命の種）... I mean seriously.

Article: Official Name for Third Impact 01
[]: Official Name for Third Impact 01?             Quote Post by Furu » Sun Mar 16, 2008 7:48 am So I've been playing around with my Super Robot Wars games lately (part of the reason I was inspired to google "Eva wiki" and sign up here actually) and after coming to the End of Evangelion bit in Alpha 3, I noticed that the game actually gave a special name to the Third Impact version of EVA-01 with the Spear and the whole cross thing/wings of light. The translation of the name I have is "Yorishiro EVA-01". A quick bit of google research shows that it's a Shinto reference: The place (or object or person) inhabited by a kami's spirit when it descends for a religious ceremony or when possessing a person.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Wed Jun 25, 2008 8:28 am This is worth looking at. What spirit has EVA-01 become a vessel for during 3I?

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Jun 25, 2008 8:58 am heh, in b4 rampant "Yui became Seed of Life" theories

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Wed Jun 25, 2008 10:12 am afaik the entire Seed of Life stuff rests on Reichu's translation of something from a videogame. Who knows what was changed during the translation or whether there is some, intentional or not, bias in leaning towards one interpretation in the translated text.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jun 25, 2008 11:34 am There really aren't many ways one can translate Seimei no Tane （生命の種）... I mean seriously.
